commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] This PR needs author code changes plus maintainer trust acceptance and draft handling before merge, so the next action is human PR review rather than a standalone cleanup close.; Needs attention: The patch crosses an external MCP media trust boundary and still lacks an aggregate budget for untrusted binary staging.; Review finding: [P1] Bound total MCP media staging per tool result — src/agents/agent-bundle-m... (sha=24abea71263b203f0030d74004458590d96cf8f5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR stages MCP image, audio, and blob-backed resource content into outbound media attachments, exposes them through `details.media.attachments`, and lets host-staged MCP local paths pass the external tool-result media filter.

PR surface: Source +219, Tests +164. Total +383 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the current review finding by source inspection: PR head maps every MCP content block into parallel staging and flattens all attachments with no result-level count or aggregate byte budget. I did not run tests in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This PR needs author code changes plus maintainer trust acceptance and draft handling before merge, so the next action is human PR review rather than a standalone cleanup close.

target.title: [codex] feat(mcp): stage binary tool content for relay
target.body:
## Summary

Part of #87023.

This adds a conservative relay foundation for MCP binary content:

- stage MCP `image`, `audio`, and blob-backed `resource` content into OpenClaw-owned outbound media attachments
- expose those staged files through `details.media.attachments` so the existing channel relay extractor can hand them off
- add a process-local host-owned MCP media registry so only files staged by OpenClaw can bypass the external-tool local-path filter

The model-facing transcript behavior stays compatible: valid MCP images still remain image blocks, while audio/resource blocks continue to degrade to text summaries for providers that do not accept those block types.

## Safety

- MCP-provenance arbitrary local paths remain blocked by the existing external-tool filter.
- MCP servers cannot bless their own local paths; only host-staged media paths are registered.
- Staging uses the existing outbound media store and media-core MIME/size caps.
- Staging failures degrade to the existing tool-result content instead of failing the MCP tool call.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `git diff --check`

`$autoreview` was requested by the contributor guide, but no autoreview tool is available in this Codex session.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: MCP `ImageContent` and blob-backed binary `Content` from a real MCP stdio server are preserved as OpenClaw-owned outbound media attachments and then relayed through the existing tool-result media extractor/filter. This exercises the #87023 foundation path without trusting MCP-supplied local paths.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at commit `25ca3ae` on macOS, Node `25.9.0`, isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.ha2Z0P/openclaw.json`. `pnpm openclaw --version` started the real CLI and reported `OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (25ca3ae)`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran `pnpm openclaw --version`, then ran a live `node --import tsx` smoke that started a local stdio MCP server with `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, connected via OpenClaw `createSessionMcpRuntime`, materialized the MCP tool through `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun`, executed it, and passed the result through `extractToolResultMediaArtifact` plus `filterToolResultMediaUrls`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the live OpenClaw smoke:

```text
OPENCLAW_REAL_MCP_RELAY_PROOF_START
openclaw_config_path=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.ha2Z0P/openclaw.json
openclaw_state_dir=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.ha2Z0P
media_dir=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.ha2Z0P/media
catalog_servers=binaryProof
catalog_tools=binaryProof/relay_binary
materialized_tool=binaryProof__relay_binary
model_content_types=text,image,text
details_media_source=mcp
details_media_host_owned=true
attachment_count=2
attachment=resource:application/pdf:binaryProof-relay_binary-2---74a8179f-2120-470c-8e42-c37a2c0dab56.pdf
attachment=image:image/png:binaryProof-relay_binary-1---dc17c5fd-402c-48da-82c3-1439606af81e.png
extractor_media_count=2
relay_filtered_count=2
staged_file=binaryProof-relay_binary-2---74a8179f-2120-470c-8e42-c37a2c0dab56.pdf:exists=true:bytes=133
staged_file=binaryProof-relay_binary-1---dc17c5fd-402c-48da-82c3-1439606af81e.png:exists=true:bytes=68
spoofed_local_mcp_path_allowed=0
OPENCLAW_REAL_MCP_RELAY_PROOF_END
```

- **Observed result after fix**: OpenClaw connected to the real MCP stdio server, listed `binaryProof/relay_binary`, executed it, staged one `application/pdf` resource and one `image/png` image into the isolated OpenClaw media store, exposed both through `details.media` with `source=mcp` and `hostOwned=true`, and the relay filter allowed exactly those two host-staged attachments while rejecting a spoofed MCP local path.
- **What was not tested**: Native Telegram/iMessage/Signal delivery was not exercised in this local proof; this PR intentionally stops at the shared OpenClaw MCP-to-tool-result media relay seam and leaves channel-specific UX/product behavior for follow-up review.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After the staging safety/order fixes, MCP `ImageContent` and blob-backed binary `Content` from a real MCP stdio server are preserved as OpenClaw-owned outbound media attachments in MCP content order, then relayed through the existing tool-result media extractor/filter. The same run verifies that unstaged MCP local paths are still rejected.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at commit `826e0d4` on macOS, Node `25.9.0`, isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.xsbghg/openclaw.json`. `pnpm openclaw --version` started the real CLI and reported `OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (826e0d4)`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran `pnpm openclaw --version`, then ran a live `node --import tsx` smoke that started a local stdio MCP server with `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, connected via OpenClaw `createSessionMcpRuntime`, materialized the MCP tool through `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun`, executed it, and passed the result through `extractToolResultMediaArtifact` plus `filterToolResultMediaUrls`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the live OpenClaw smoke at the latest pushed head:

```text
OPENCLAW_REAL_MCP_RELAY_PROOF_START
openclaw_config_path=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.xsbghg/openclaw.json
openclaw_state_dir=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.xsbghg
media_dir=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.xsbghg/media
catalog_servers=binaryProof
catalog_tools=binaryProof/relay_binary
materialized_tool=binaryProof__relay_binary
model_content_types=text,image,text
details_media_source=mcp
details_media_host_owned=true
attachment_count=2
attachment=image:image/png:binaryProof-relay_binary-1---35f3e99e-da57-4359-8716-d598fd7e0ab7.png
attachment=resource:application/pdf:binaryProof-relay_binary-2---5507978b-26eb-4816-a43b-8b9ae740cd6c.pdf
extractor_media_count=2
relay_filtered_count=2
staged_file=binaryProof-relay_binary-1---35f3e99e-da57-4359-8716-d598fd7e0ab7.png:exists=true:bytes=68
staged_file=binaryProof-relay_binary-2---5507978b-26eb-4816-a43b-8b9ae740cd6c.pdf:exists=true:bytes=133
spoofed_local_mcp_path_allowed=0
OPENCLAW_REAL_MCP_RELAY_PROOF_END
```

- **Observed result after fix**: OpenClaw connected to the real MCP stdio server, listed `binaryProof/relay_binary`, executed it, staged one `image/png` image and one `application/pdf` resource into the isolated OpenClaw media store, exposed both through `details.media` with `source=mcp` and `hostOwned=true`, preserved content order (`image` before `resource`), and the relay filter allowed exactly those two host-staged attachments while rejecting a spoofed MCP local path.
- **What was not tested**: Native Telegram/iMessage/Signal delivery was not exercised in this local proof; this PR intentionally stops at the shared OpenClaw MCP-to-tool-result media relay seam and leaves channel-specific UX/product behavior for follow-up review.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After squashing the final safety/order fixes into a single head commit, MCP `ImageContent` and blob-backed binary `Content` from a real MCP stdio server are preserved as OpenClaw-owned outbound media attachments in MCP content order, then relayed through the existing tool-result media extractor/filter. The same run verifies that unstaged MCP local paths are still rejected.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at commit `24abea7` on macOS, Node `25.9.0`, isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.Ieyv0f/openclaw.json`. `pnpm openclaw --version` started the real CLI and reported `OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (24abea7)`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran `pnpm openclaw --version`, then ran a live `node --import tsx` smoke that started a local stdio MCP server with `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, connected via OpenClaw `createSessionMcpRuntime`, materialized the MCP tool through `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun`, executed it, and passed the result through `extractToolResultMediaArtifact` plus `filterToolResultMediaUrls`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the live OpenClaw smoke at the latest pushed head:

```text
OPENCLAW_REAL_MCP_RELAY_PROOF_START
openclaw_config_path=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.Ieyv0f/openclaw.json
openclaw_state_dir=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.Ieyv0f
media_dir=/tmp/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.Ieyv0f/media
catalog_servers=binaryProof
catalog_tools=binaryProof/relay_binary
materialized_tool=binaryProof__relay_binary
model_content_types=text,image,text
details_media_source=mcp
details_media_host_owned=true
attachment_count=2
attachment=image:image/png:binaryProof-relay_binary-1---bac09f28-72ce-477b-b885-65c13ed05c78.png
attachment=resource:application/pdf:binaryProof-relay_binary-2---e70ad5c5-af58-4265-aac1-7c745bbdae1d.pdf
extractor_media_count=2
relay_filtered_count=2
staged_file=binaryProof-relay_binary-1---bac09f28-72ce-477b-b885-65c13ed05c78.png:exists=true:bytes=68
staged_file=binaryProof-relay_binary-2---e70ad5c5-af58-4265-aac1-7c745bbdae1d.pdf:exists=true:bytes=133
spoofed_local_mcp_path_allowed=0
OPENCLAW_REAL_MCP_RELAY_PROOF_END
```

- **Observed result after fix**: OpenClaw connected to the real MCP stdio server, listed `binaryProof/relay_binary`, executed it, staged one `image/png` image and one `application/pdf` resource into the isolated OpenClaw media store, exposed both through `details.media` with `source=mcp` and `hostOwned=true`, preserved content order (`image` before `resource`), and the relay filter allowed exactly those two host-staged attachments while rejecting a spoofed MCP local path.
- **What was not tested**: Native Telegram/iMessage/Signal delivery was not exercised in this local proof; this PR intentionally stops at the shared OpenClaw MCP-to-tool-result media relay seam and leaves channel-specific UX/product behavior for follow-up review.
